76020.subject: ūüü™ The Chicago Plan Comeback
76020.body:

----------

Caption: 

==‚ÄúMake money independent of loans.‚ÄĚ==
_‚ÄĒ _==_Irving Fisher_==



----------View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/0c7a8a1e-f64e-4940-af8c-728c30439d7b/image.png?t=1784150831)
Caption: 

# The Chicago Plan Comeback

In the depths of the Great Depression, every idea about money was on the table.

During the 1933 bank holiday, FDR proposed converting every government bond into cash at par ‚ÄĒ a singularity of debt monetization, so to speak.

His advisors considered having the Federal Reserve print enough new notes to match all bank deposits, so banks could meet withdrawal demands when they reopened.

How wide open was the Overton Window? A year later, FDR appointed Marriner Eccles to lead the Federal Reserve. Eccles, a self-made banker, had only a high-school education.

The most radical proposal, however, came from the pinnacle of academia. The Chicago Plan, proposed by economists at the University of Chicago, would have ended banking as we know it.

Their idea was to abolish fractional reserve banking.

Frank Knight, a co-author of the Chicago Plan, warned that ‚Äúimportant evils result‚ÄĚ when commercial banks are allowed to create money ‚ÄĒ "notably the frightful instability of the whole economic system and its periodical collapse in crises."

By combining the creation of money with the extension of credit, the academics argued, fractional-reserve banking tends to inflate bubbles on the way up and incite panic on the way down.

Worse still, we _pay_ the banks to provide this destabilizing service.

‚ÄúIt is absurd and monstrous for society to pay the commercial banking system ‚Äėinterest‚Äô for multiplying several fold the quantity of medium of exchange,‚ÄĚ Knight added.

The Chicago Plan would have turned that arrangement upside down: instead of everyone paying banks to create money for us, banks would pay the government to create it for them.

That, at least, is how the authors of an [IMF paper](https://www.fairmoney.info/wp-content/uploads/2016/12/IMF-The-Chicago-Plan-revisited.pdf?utm_source=chatgpt.com) from 2012 envisioned the Chicago Plan playing out: If banks suddenly needed government-issued money to back all the deposits they had created, they‚Äôd have to borrow it from its source: the go

76014.subject: ūüĒ∑ The Daily: Tether freezes $131 million tied to Iran
76014.body:
DTCC begins first tokenized stock and Treasury production trades involving JPMorgan, BlackRock and Goldman | Japan passes key bill recognizing crypto as financial product, lowering tax rate | South Korea to bring crypto under new state asset management law | Bitmine says ETH staking generated $45.7 million, accounting for 98% of quarterly revenue |

The Daily ( https://www.theblock.co/?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

Written by James Hunt | Edited by Jason Shubnell

Happy Wednesday! *Bitcoin's* push toward $65,000 could set up a run to $70,000-$75,000 by month-end as cooler inflation and easing geopolitical tensions align, *21Shares* Senior Crypto Research Strategist *Matt Mena* told The Block.

In today's newsletter, *OFAC* freezes $130 million in crypto, the *DTCC* begins its first limited production trades of real-world assets, *Japan* recognizes crypto as a financial product, and more.

Meanwhile, *Coinbase's Jesse Pollak* handed ( https://www.theblock.co/post/408504/coinbases-jesse-pollak-hands-base-app-leadership-cobie-social-bets-fell-short?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) *Base App* leadership over to *Cobie* after admitting its social bets had fallen short.

*P.S.* Don't forget to check out The Funding , a biweekly rundown of crypto VC trends. It's a great read ‚ÄĒ and just like The Daily , it's free to subscribe ( https://www.theblock.co/newsletters?select=The%20Funding&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) !

*THE BIG ONE*

*ūüáģūüá∑ US Treasury freezes over $130 million tied to Iran-linked crypto wallets*

( https://www.theblock.co/post/408357/us-treasury-freezes-130-million-iran-linked-wallets?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

The *U.S. Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control* sanctioned multiple crypto wallets tied to Iran, freezing more than $130 million, according to Treasury Secretary *Scott Bessent*.

* The wallets were targeted as part of the Treasury's effort to disrupt what Bessent called Iran's abuse of digital assets.
* Onchain analysts said *Tether* froze four *Tron* wallets holding a combined $131 million in *USDT* minutes before Bessent's post.
* The addresses were tied to the *Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps* and the *Central Bank of Iran* , with most funds withdrawn from *DTC Pay* and crypto exchange *Bitso* , they said.
* The action follows a string of large USDT freezes, including more than $344 million across two Tron addresses flagged by U.S. authorities in April.
* Tether said at the time it had frozen more than $4.4 billion in assets to date, including over $2.1 billion tied to U.S. authorities, working with 340-plus law enforcement agencies acro

76009.subject: Robinhood‚Äôs Crypto Chain Explodes
76009.body:
----------
_**Your Weekly Deep Dive into The World of Crypto**_

**July 15, 2026 ¬∑ ****5 Min Read**

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/26f99b56-9751-4856-9ade-2b8ce455b7c7/image.png?t=1784096997)
Caption: **Live Market Snapshot | As of July 15, 2026 | Sources: CoinDesk, CryptoBriefing, CoinMarketCap, Alternative.me, SoSoValue**

## ūüď°** THIS WEEK IN CRYPTO**

**GM, Readers!**

Chaos week. Trump called the Iran ceasefire dead Wednesday, and $450M in leveraged crypto evaporated. By Friday, Bitcoin ETFs printed their first green week in two months, Robinhood launched a chain that became a memecoin casino overnight, and hackers ran a rug through a hijacked SpaceX X account.

BTC opened near $63,293, hit $61,492 (low) on July 8, then clawed above $64,600 by Friday on a short squeeze. Every degen who missed CASHCAT is asking what a Robinhood Chain wallet is.¬†

_**Let‚Äôs get into it...**_


----------View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/6c53930f-b4d7-4e7d-baee-6a88bb56a6da/image.png?t=1781097813)
Caption: 

----------
# ūüźč THE BIG WHALE MOVES

### **1. Robinhood Built a Blockchain for Stocks. Degens Built a Cat Coin.**

Robinhood Chain[ went live July 1](https://robinhood.com/us/en/newsroom/robinhood-accelerates-global-expansion-robinhood-chain-mainnet-stock-tokens-agentic-trading/) as an Arbitrum L2 pitched at tokenized equities. Within nine days,[ daily volume hit $568M](https://www.coindesk.com/markets/2026/07/09/arbitrum-jumps-19-benefitting-from-robinhood-s-usd568-million-onchain-trading-frenzy), with 75%+ memecoin activity. ARB[ jumped 19%](https://www.techtimes.com/articles/320117/20260710/robinhood-chains-568m-day-sends-arb-soaring-fee-revenue-blueprint.htm) on the news, and a cat token called[ CASHCAT](https://fortune.com/crypto/2026/07/13/robinhood-chain-memecoin-trading-cash-cat-vlad-tenev-crypto/) briefly hit a $150M market cap. CEO Vlad Tenev leaned in: "It works great for memes, too."

Then it turned. Protos reported honeypots, phishing links, and wallet drainers, with one CASHCAT holder reporting a[ $56K honeypot loss per TechTimes and Protos](https://www.techtimes.com/articles/320117/20260710/robinhood-chains-568m-day-sends-arb-soaring-fee-revenue-blueprint.htm). On July 12, hackers used SpaceX accounts to promote a rug (below).

_**Translation: **__Robinhood built rails for Wall Street. Degens showed up first. So did scammers._

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/75a266e1-81f3-4c8c-8d8d-b120ed6856d0/image.png?t=1784097053)
Caption: 

### **2. Tom Lee's BitMine Is 0.2% Away From Owning 5% of All ETH**

BitMine (BMNR)[ added another 40,000 ETH](https://x.com/lookonchain/status/2074684833897603145) on July 8 via FalconX and Kraken for about $71.6M, following a[ disclosed](

Brooke Unger

Senior digital editor



 China gives the impression that its open-source artificial-intelligence models are more cuddly than America’s domineering proprietary ones.  And so everybody that is not America should adopt its technology.

 warns The Telegram, our geopolitics column.
